children.heading: Computerized statewide voter registration list requirements
children.label: a Computerized statewide voter registration list requirements
i
children.text: The computerized list shall serve as the single system for storing and managing the official list of registered voters throughout the State.

children.label: ii
children.text: The computerized list contains the name and registration information of every legally registered voter in the State.

children.label: iii
children.text: Under the computerized list, a unique identifier is assigned to each legally registered voter in the State.

children.label: iv
children.text: The computerized list shall be coordinated with other agency databases within the State.

children.label: v
children.text: Any election official in the State, including any local election official, may obtain immediate electronic access to the information contained in the computerized list.

children.label: vi
children.text: All voter registration information obtained by any local election official in the State shall be electronically entered into the computerized list on an expedited basis at the time the information is provided to the local official.

children.label: vii
children.text: The chief State election official shall provide such support as may be required so that local election officials are able to enter information as described in clause (vi).

children.label: viii
children.text: The computerized list shall serve as the official voter registration list for the conduct of all elections for Federal office in the State.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The requirement under subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a State in which, under a State law in effect continuously on and after , there is no voter registration requirement for individuals in the State with respect to elections for Federal office.October 29, 2002

children.label: i
children.text: If an individual is to be removed from the computerized list, such individual shall be removed in accordance with the provisions of the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 ( et seq.) [now et seq.], including subsections (a)(4), (c)(2), (d), and (e) of section 8 of such Act () [now ].42 U.S.C. 1973gg52 U.S.C. 2050142 U.S.C. 1973gg–652 U.S.C. 20507

children.label: I
children.text: under section 8(a)(3)(B) of such Act () [now ], the State shall coordinate the computerized list with State agency records on felony status; and42 U.S.C. 1973gg–6(a)(3)(B)52 U.S.C. 20507(a)(3)(B)

children.label: II
children.text: by reason of the death of the registrant under section 8(a)(4)(A) of such Act () [now ], the State shall coordinate the computerized list with State agency records on death.42 U.S.C. 1973gg–6(a)(4)(A)52 U.S.C. 20507(a)(4)(A)

children.label: iii
children.text: Notwithstanding the preceding provisions of this subparagraph, if a State is described in section 4(b) of the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 () [now ], that State shall remove the names of ineligible voters from the computerized list in accordance with State law.42 U.S.C. 1973gg–2(b)52 U.S.C. 20503(b)

children.label: i
children.text: the name of each registered voter appears in the computerized list;

children.label: ii
children.text: only voters who are not registered or who are not eligible to vote are removed from the computerized list; and

children.label: iii
children.text: duplicate names are eliminated from the computerized list.

children.heading: Technological security of computerized list
children.label: 3 Technological security of computerized list
children.text: The appropriate State or local official shall provide adequate technological security measures to prevent the unauthorized access to the computerized list established under this section.

children.heading: Minimum standard for accuracy of State voter registration records
children.label: 4 Minimum standard for accuracy of State voter registration records
A
children.text: A system of file maintenance that makes a reasonable effort to remove registrants who are ineligible to vote from the official list of eligible voters. Under such system, consistent with the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 ( et seq.) [now et seq.], registrants who have not responded to a notice and who have not voted in 2 consecutive general elections for Federal office shall be removed from the official list of eligible voters, except that no registrant may be removed solely by reason of a failure to vote.42 U.S.C. 1973gg52 U.S.C. 20501

children.label: B
children.text: Safeguards to ensure that eligible voters are not removed in error from the official list of eligible voters.

children.heading: Requiring provision of certain information by applicants
children.label: A Requiring provision of certain information by applicants
I
children.text: in the case of an applicant who has been issued a current and valid driver’s license, the applicant’s driver’s license number; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any other applicant (other than an applicant to whom clause (ii) applies), the last 4 digits of the applicant’s social security number.

children.heading: Special rule for applicants without driver’s license or social security number
children.label: ii Special rule for applicants without driver’s license or social security number
children.text: If an applicant for voter registration for an election for Federal office has not been issued a current and valid driver’s license or a social security number, the State shall assign the applicant a number which will serve to identify the applicant for voter registration purposes. To the extent that the State has a computerized list in effect under this subsection and the list assigns unique identifying numbers to registrants, the number assigned under this clause shall be the unique identifying number assigned under the list.

children.heading: Determination of validity of numbers provided
children.label: iii Determination of validity of numbers provided
children.text: The State shall determine whether the information provided by an individual is sufficient to meet the requirements of this subparagraph, in accordance with State law.

children.heading: Sharing information in databases
children.label: i Sharing information in databases
children.text: The chief State election official and the official responsible for the State motor vehicle authority of a State shall enter into an agreement to match information in the database of the statewide voter registration system with information in the database of the motor vehicle authority to the extent required to enable each such official to verify the accuracy of the information provided on applications for voter registration.

children.heading: Agreements with Commissioner of Social Security
children.label: ii Agreements with Commissioner of Social Security
children.text: The official responsible for the State motor vehicle authority shall enter into an agreement with the Commissioner of Social Security under section 405(r)(8) of title 42 (as added by subparagraph (C)).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Special rule for certain States
children.label: D Special rule for certain States
children.text: In the case of a State which is permitted to use social security numbers, and provides for the use of social security numbers, on applications for voter registration, in accordance with section 7 of the Privacy Act of 1974 ( note), the provisions of this paragraph shall be optional.5 U.S.C. 552a

children.heading: Requirements for voters who register by mail
children.label: b Requirements for voters who register by mail
A
children.text: the individual registered to vote in a jurisdiction by mail; and

children.label: i
children.text: the individual has not previously voted in an election for Federal office in the State; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual has not previously voted in such an election in the jurisdiction and the jurisdiction is located in a State that does not have a computerized list that complies with the requirements of subsection (a).

children.label: I
children.text: presents to the appropriate State or local election official a current and valid photo identification; or

children.label: II
children.text: presents to the appropriate State or local election official a copy of a current utility bill, bank statement, government check, paycheck, or other government document that shows the name and address of the voter; or

children.label: I
children.text: a copy of a current and valid photo identification; or

children.label: II
children.text: a copy of a current utility bill, bank statement, government check, paycheck, or other government document that shows the name and address of the voter.

children.heading: In person
children.label: i In person
children.text: An individual who desires to vote in person, but who does not meet the requirements of subparagraph (A)(i), may cast a provisional ballot under .section 21082(a) of this title

children.heading: By mail
children.label: ii By mail
children.text: An individual who desires to vote by mail but who does not meet the requirements of subparagraph (A)(ii) may cast such a ballot by mail and the ballot shall be counted as a provisional ballot in accordance with .section 21082(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a copy of a current and valid photo identification; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a copy of a current utility bill, bank statement, government check, paycheck, or government document that shows the name and address of the voter;

children.label: I
children.text: a driver’s license number; or

children.label: II
children.text: at least the last 4 digits of the individual’s social security number; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to whom a State or local election official matches the information submitted under clause (i) with an existing State identification record bearing the same number, name and date of birth as provided in such registration; or

children.label: i
children.text: entitled to vote by absentee ballot under the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act [ et seq.];52 U.S.C. 20301

children.label: ii
children.text: provided the right to vote otherwise than in person under ; orsection 20102(b)(2)(B)(ii) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: entitled to vote otherwise than in person under any other Federal law.

children.label: i
children.text: The question “Are you a citizen of the United States of America?” and boxes for the applicant to check to indicate whether the applicant is or is not a citizen of the United States.

children.label: ii
children.text: The question “Will you be 18 years of age on or before election day?” and boxes for the applicant to check to indicate whether or not the applicant will be 18 years of age or older on election day.

children.label: iii
children.text: The statement “If you checked ‘no’ in response to either of these questions, do not complete this form.”.

children.label: iv
children.text: A statement informing the individual that if the form is submitted by mail and the individual is registering for the first time, the appropriate information required under this section must be submitted with the mail-in registration form in order to avoid the additional identification requirements upon voting for the first time.

children.heading: Incomplete forms
children.label: B Incomplete forms
children.text: If an applicant for voter registration fails to answer the question included on the mail voter registration form pursuant to subparagraph (A)(i), the registrar shall notify the applicant of the failure and provide the applicant with an opportunity to complete the form in a timely manner to allow for the completion of the registration form prior to the next election for Federal office (subject to State law).

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 5 Construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to require a State that was not required to comply with a provision of the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 ( et seq.) [now et seq.] before , to comply with such a provision after .42 U.S.C. 1973gg52 U.S.C. 20501October 29, 2002October 29, 2002

children.heading: Permitted use of last 4 digits of social security numbers
children.label: c Permitted use of last 4 digits of social security numbers
children.text: The last 4 digits of a social security number described in subsections (a)(5)(A)(i)(II) and (b)(3)(B)(i)(II) shall not be considered to be a social security number for purposes of section 7 of the Privacy Act of 1974 ( note).5 U.S.C. 552a

children.heading: Computerized statewide voter registration list requirements
children.label: 1 Computerized statewide voter registration list requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), each State and jurisdiction shall be required to comply with the requirements of subsection (a) on and after .January 1, 2004

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: B Waiver
children.text: If a State or jurisdiction certifies to the Commission not later than , that the State or jurisdiction will not meet the deadline described in subparagraph (A) for good cause and includes in the certification the reasons for the failure to meet such deadline, subparagraph (A) shall apply to the State or jurisdiction as if the reference in such subparagraph to “” were a reference to “”.January 1, 2004January 1, 2004January 1, 2006

children.heading: Requirement for voters who register by mail
children.label: 2 Requirement for voters who register by mail

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each State and jurisdiction shall be required to comply with the requirements of subsection (b) on and after , and shall be prepared to receive registration materials submitted by individuals described in subparagraph (B) on and after the date described in such subparagraph.January 1, 2004

children.heading: Applicability with respect to individuals
children.label: B Applicability with respect to individuals
children.text: The provisions of subsection (b) shall apply to any individual who registers to vote on or after .January 1, 2003
